figures.caption: Figure 4: Ring implementation of All-Gather operation showing example for four compute ranks.
Figure 5: Ring implementation of Reduce-Scatter operation showing example for four compute ranks.
Figure 1: A graphical example showing how the proposed solution can hide the communication overhead under computation and hence overcome the limitations of state of the art approaches.
Figure 8: Comparison across sequence lengths. Left: Communication Overhead Reduction. Right: End-toEnd Latency Improvement.
Table 3: Communication overhead reduction (%) and end-to-end latency improvement (%) compared to the vanilla baseline across sequence lengths.
Table 2: Example resized table with fixed column widths

Figure 6: Four-rank example of Flash All Gather, showing the ring-ordered breakdown of the collective into peer-to-peer communication steps with interleaved partial-output computation to achieve compute–communication overlap.
Figure 9: Example of decomposed query split attention for Flash-RS showing the benefit of asynchronous P2P decomposed communication eliminating communication overhead.

Figure 7: Four-rank example of Flash Reduce Scatter, showing the ring-ordered breakdown of the Reduce Scatter collective into peer-to-peer communication steps with interleaved partial-output computation to achieve compute–communication overlap.
Figure 2: All Gather (AG) operation showing initial state and communication pattern on the left and final state on the right.
Figure 3: Reduce-Scatter (RS) operation showing initial state and communication pattern on the left and final state on the right.
